key_insight: A first-principles nonequilibrium BSE plus ab initio molecular dynamics framework can separate electronic and thermal lattice effects in transient absorption of CH3NH3PbBr3 across femtosecond-to-picosecond timescales.
engineering_relevance: The method improves interpretation of pump-probe spectroscopy in soft perovskites, helping engineers connect measured spectral shifts to specific ultrafast mechanisms.
method: Nonequilibrium extension of the Bethe-Salpeter equation for excited-state spectra
Ab initio molecular dynamics to capture thermal lattice motion
Comparative analysis of X-ray and optical transient absorption across timescales
evidence_signal: The abstract reports quantitative agreement in disentangling transient absorption into electronic-dominated femtosecond responses and lattice-dominated picosecond responses, with distinct mechanisms for X-ray versus optical spectra.
watch_item: The approach is computationally intensive and may be challenging to transfer to larger systems or longer timescales without approximations.
paper_title: Disentangling Electronic and Lattice Contributions to Transient Absorption in Metal Halide Perovskites: A First-Principles Study of CH3NH3PbBr3
